question_text: A magnetic field is represented by lines drawn around a magnet. What do the arrows on these field lines indicate?

options.label: A
options.text: The direction of electric current flowing through the magnet

options.label: B
options.text: The direction from south pole to north pole

options.label: C
options.text: The direction from north pole to south pole

options.label: D
options.text: The strength of the magnetic field in different regions

explanation: Magnetic field lines show the direction of the magnetic field, which is defined as pointing from the south pole to the north pole outside the magnet (and from north to south inside the magnet).
